title: Native Americans Fought in the Revolution to Save Their Land — But Lost It Anyway
summary:
Imagine you're a Native American kid in 1777. Your family has lived on the same land for centuries. Then a war breaks out between the British and the American colonists. Both sides want you to fight for them. What do you do?

That's what happened to the Haudenosaunee Confederacy (say: ho-dee-no-SHOW-nee), a group of six Native nations in what is now New York. At first, they tried to stay out of the American Revolution. But the British and the colonists kept pushing them. So the Oneida and Tuscarora chose the colonists, while the Mohawk, Onondaga, Cayuga, and Seneca chose the British.

This split led to a terrible battle at Oriskany on August 6, 1777. For the first time in hundreds of years, Haudenosaunee people fought each other. "Those scars cut deep," says historian Alexis Albright.

Why did Native nations join the war? Not because they liked one side better. They wanted to protect their homelands from settlers who kept moving onto their land. "It's really a war about land," says historian Heather Bruegl.

But after the Revolution, the new United States took Native land anyway. Many Native people were forced to move west. The war they fought to save their homes ended up costing them everything.
why_it_matters: This story reminds us that even when people try to do the right thing for their families and homes, sometimes the outcome is unfair, and it's important to remember those who lost.

keywords.explanation: A group of six Native American nations in what is now New York.
One of the six Haudenosaunee nations that chose to support the American colonists.
One of the six Haudenosaunee nations that chose to support the British.
People from the colonies who moved onto Native American land.
The war between the American colonies and Great Britain from 1775 to 1783.

questions.question: What did the Haudenosaunee Confederacy want to do at the start of the war?
Which two Haudenosaunee nations fought for the American colonists?
What happened on August 6, 1777?
options: The Battle of Oriskany was fought
questions.correct_answer: The Battle of Oriskany was fought

questions.question: Why did Native nations join the war, according to historian Heather Bruegl?
options: Because they liked the British more
To protect their homelands from settlers
questions.correct_answer: To protect their homelands from settlers

questions.question: What happened to Native land after the American Revolution?
options: The United States took it anyway
It was returned to Native people
questions.correct_answer: The United States took it anyway

questions.question: How many nations are in the Haudenosaunee Confederacy?

background_read: The American Revolution (1775-1783) was a war between Great Britain and the 13 American colonies.
Many Native American nations lived in the Northeast and were caught in the middle.
The Haudenosaunee Confederacy was one of the oldest and most powerful alliances of Native nations in North America.
Article_Structure: WHO: Haudenosaunee people (especially the Oneida, Tuscarora, Mohawk, Onondaga, Cayuga, Seneca).
WHAT: They fought in the American Revolution to protect their land but lost it after the war.
WHERE: New York, especially at the Battle of Oriskany.
WHY: The British and colonists pressured them, and they chose sides to protect their homelands from settlers.

perspectives.description: They had to make a difficult choice between two sides in a war they didn't start, hoping to protect their land, but lost it anyway.
They saw Native land as theirs for the taking because they wanted more space to live and farm.
They say the American Revolution was not just about independence but really about land and who gets to control it.
